headline: Oklo Meta Deal Links Early Nuclear Funding To Future Data Center Demand
summary: Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) has signed a prepayment power agreement with Meta Platforms for electricity from its planned Ohio nuclear plant. The arrangement is intended to fund the initial stages of the project, including pre construction and site characterization work targeted for 2026. The deal links Oklo's planned small modular reactor output to future energy needs for Meta's operations, including data centers. Oklo is developing small modular nuclear reactors aimed at providing reliable, carbon...

headline: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg says at consumer protection trial that he resisted censoring platforms
summary: Jurors in a bellwether trial about the impacts of social media on teenagers and children on Wednesday watched a deposition of Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg that explores what the architects of Facebook and Instagram knew from internal research about the negative experiences by young users and how the company responded since its early years.  Prosecutors are alleging that Meta violated state consumer protection laws in failing to disclose what it knew about the dangers of addiction to social media as well as child sexual exploitation on the company's platforms, while attorneys for Meta say the company discloses risks, makes efforts to weed out harmful content and experiences, and acknowledges that some bad material still gets through its safety net.

raw.headline: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg says at consumer protection trial that he resisted censoring platforms
raw.summary: Jurors in a bellwether trial about the impacts of social media on teenagers and children on Wednesday watched a deposition of Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg that explores what the architects of Facebook and Instagram knew from internal research about the negative experiences by young users and how the company responded since its early years.  Prosecutors are alleging that Meta violated state consumer protection laws in failing to disclose what it knew about the dangers of addiction to social media as well as child sexual exploitation on the company's platforms, while attorneys for Meta say the company discloses risks, makes efforts to weed out harmful content and experiences, and acknowledges that some bad material still gets through its safety net.

headline: Broadcom sees revenue above estimates as AI fuels custom chip demand
summary: Chip designer Broadcom forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, a sign of strong demand for advanced chips ‌used in data centers powering artificial intelligence applications.  Big Tech firms such ‌as Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta are expected to spend at least $630 billion to build AI ​infrastructure this year, which would boost demand for chips, servers, storage and networking equipment from companies like Broadcom.  "Our AI revenue growth is accelerating, and we expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2," CEO Hock Tan said in a statement.

raw.headline: Broadcom sees revenue above estimates as AI fuels custom chip demand
raw.summary: Chip designer Broadcom forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, a sign of strong demand for advanced chips ‌used in data centers powering artificial intelligence applications.  Big Tech firms such ‌as Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta are expected to spend at least $630 billion to build AI ​infrastructure this year, which would boost demand for chips, servers, storage and networking equipment from companies like Broadcom.  "Our AI revenue growth is accelerating, and we expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2," CEO Hock Tan said in a statement.

headline: Whoop to Expand Staff by 75% to Spur Growth Ahead of Likely IPO
summary: The Boston-based company intends to add more than 600 new roles across various departments, including software, research, design, hardware, product development, manufacturing, sales and marketing, founder and Chief Executive Office Will Ahmed said in an interview announcing the expansion.  The majority of the new employees will be based at the company’s headquarters, he said, with additional hiring planned across North America, Europe, Asia and Gulf Cooperation Council nations, including Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

summary: SANTA MONICA, Calif., March 04, 2026--Binarly, the industry leader in software and firmware supply‑chain security, today announced a leadership transition as the company enters its next phase of growth. Founder and current CEO Alex Matrosov has joined the company’s Board, and Gwenyth Castro has joined as Chief Executive Officer to scale global go-to-market and enterprise growth.

headline: Soaring Power Prices Have US Politicians Searching for Solutions
summary: The Energy Department has loaned $26.5 billion — the most in its history — to help a big Southeastern utility reduce the cost of building new power plants in Georgia and Alabama.  Meanwhile, the nation’s biggest grid operator has proposed capping wholesale prices and some governors are weighing utility rate freezes as a way to keep a lid on electricity costs.  The affordability problem is prompting a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives.

The affordability problem is prompting a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives.
